prompt: For a protein engineering research program, optimize the half-life at 37°C of Tetanus toxin (tetanospasmin) (bacterial zinc metalloprotease neurotoxin (toxin)) from Clostridium tetani. specify a computational protocol for: (1) stability hotspot identification, (2) point mutation screening using FoldX or Rosetta, (3) combinatorial variant prioritization. Include relevant computational tools and databases to use.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: Tetanus toxin (tetanospasmin); organism: Clostridium tetani; catalogue version v1.0
